name: Whole Wheat Turnovers ( September 1992)
recipeInstructions:
Hearts of Palm Filling
2 tablespoons oil
2 tablespoons flour
2 tablespoons finely chopped onion
1/2 cup milk
1 teaspoon chopped parsley
pinch nutmeg
salt and freshly ground white pepper, to taste
1 cup hearts of palms (chopped)
Cheese Filling
1 cup firm ricotta or farmers cheese
1/4 cup chopped spring onions
1 egg
salt and white pepper, to taste
Dough:
1. Place flour in a bowl.
2. Make a well in the center and add oil, salt, and a little warm water.
3. Stir with a fork and then with hands. You should have a firm, not too soft, dough.
4. Knead 5 minutes, adding more flour if needed. Let rest covered and oiled for 30 minutes.
5. Roll out dough very thin on a working surface dusted with whole wheat flour.
6. Cut out 4 rounds (approximately 4 inches). Place 2 tablespoons of filling in the center, brush edges with water, fold over filling, and press edges together firmly with a fork to seal.
7. Place turnovers on a lightly oiled cookie sheet.
8. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 25-30 minutes.
Heart of Palms Filling:
1. Cook onion in oil until softened.
2. Stir in flour and cook 2 minutes more.
3. Remove from heat, let stand 5 minutes.
4. Add milk and stir over low heat until thickened.
5. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in chopped hearts of palms and parsley. Let cool before using.
Cheese Filling:
Stir together all the ingredients. You can also make mushroom filling, chicken filling, or any other desired filling. If you want more fillings, just let me know.
